title: fumana vulgaris subsp. spachii
intro: Fumana vulgaris subsp. spachii is a subspecies of the Fumana vulgaris plant, commonly known as the daphne or shrubby daphne, a flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable resource for wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Fumana vulgaris subsp. spachii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
